replacement.description: Selector to substitute for the TBD token. Nullable when the item is an honest skip (applied=false, e.g. ghost/duplicate per dom-comparison or 'no DOM element matched' from the resolver).

strategy.description: Locator strategy when applied=true. Null on honest skips (the resolver did not pick a strategy because there was nothing real to point at).

comparison_verdict.description: Stamped by Step 8b's dom-comparison pass. ghost = no DOM element exists; duplicate = same element as another TBD; matched = real element exists. The Step 8 apply-rate gate excludes ghost/duplicate from its denominator.

snapshot_source.description: Which snapshot mechanism the playwright-tester used to evidence this resolution. `aom` is the default (`browser_snapshot` accessibility tree). `raw_dom_fallback` means the agent had to fall back to `browser_evaluate(() => document.documentElement.outerHTML)` because the element was missing from the AOM, non-semantic, or screen-reader-hidden. Populated by Step 8a; absent on older runs.

fallback_reason.description: Required when `snapshot_source = raw_dom_fallback`: one of `not_in_aom`, `non_semantic`, `aria_hidden`, or a free-text description. Lets Step 8b's audit and the report distinguish 'spec gap' from 'accessibility gap in the SUT'.

source.description: How this resolution was produced. `agent` (default, absent) means Step 8a's playwright-tester produced it. `hitl` means the user supplied the selector via the Step 8a clarification prompt after the agent declared the element unresolvable.

duplicates.description: Groups of two or more applied items that resolved to the same selector inside the same file. Surfaced as a quality signal — either the codegen step invented overlapping abstractions (e.g. GEMINI_NAV_BUTTON + GEMINI_NAV_LINK pointing at the same DOM element) or the resolver collapsed multiple TBDs onto one parent locator. Not a pipeline failure on its own.

low_confidence_masks.description: Items the resolver marked applied with confidence below the missing-element threshold AND whose selector is reused for another item in the same file. This is the signature of a silently masked spec/implementation gap — the resolver fell back to a parent/duplicate selector instead of admitting nothing matched.
